title: Career Crossroads
content: You know, I left the Federal Reserve to come to Vanguard. It wasn't obvious it was the right move - the Fed was prestigious, stable, important work. But something wasn't clicking. I realized I wanted to help people more directly. Nearly 30 years later, I'm glad I took that leap. The question I'd ask is: what's the cost of NOT making a change? Sometimes the risk of staying is higher than the risk of going. What does your gut tell you?...
follow_up: At the Fed, I saw how macroeconomic policy affected millions of people. At Vanguard, I could help individual investors directly - one person at a time, but at massive scale.
